title: Rhyan End Table
summary: A plywood end table with two euro-slide drawers behind mirrored acrylic doors — 2x2 leg framing and drawers built to your actual slide clearance rather than a fixed number.
description:
This end table's cabinet is built almost entirely from ripped 3/4" plywood panels framed in 2x2s, with two working drawers riding euro-style slides and a mirrored acrylic-panel front that gives the piece a more finished, boutique look than a typical construction-lumber build. Build three identical 2x2-framed side panels first, but only two of them become drawer-slide spacers — keep that distinction straight as you build, since all three start out looking the same.

Once the drawer-slide sides have their legs attached, the shelf and top go on, followed by the third panel at the back and 2x2 trim across the shelves and bottom — this is where the case stops being loose panels and becomes a rigid box. **Drawer sizing is genuinely build-to-fit here, not build-to-plan**: the source is explicit that drawers should always be sized to the opening you actually built, checking your specific slide hardware's clearance requirement (commonly 1/2" per side) rather than trusting the plan's stated dimensions.

The acrylic-and-mirror-clip doors are the final flourish: build the door frames from 1x2 stock, test-fit with hinges, then mount acrylic panels to the back with mirror clips and add clasps to keep the doors shut. That combination — plywood carcass, real drawers, and a glazed door front — puts this table a step up in finish complexity from most of this catalog's other end tables.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood panels to width.

materials.name: 3/4" hardwood plywood or MDF, half sheet, 48 in x 48 in or larger
Acrylic glass panels, 12 in x 12 in
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 3/4" hardwood plywood or MDF, half sheet, 48 in x 48 in or larger
3/4" hardwood plywood or MDF, half sheet, 48 in x 48 in or larger
3/4" hardwood plywood or MDF, half sheet, 48 in x 48 in or larger

cutList.part: Drawer slide spacers and drawer trim

cutList.note: Build to fit your door opening.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two shelf and top at 17", three side panels at 12-1/4" and two drawer bottoms at 14-1/2" from the 3/4" hardwood plywood or MDF, half sheet, 48 in x 48 in or larger. Cut four legs at 26" and twelve panel top/bottom framing at 17" from the 2x2. Cut five drawer slide spacers and drawer trim at 17" and eight door frame stiles/rails at 12" from the 1x2. Cut four drawer sides at 17", two drawer backs at 16" and two drawer fronts at 16-3/4" from the 1x6. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to four 8 ft 2x2, three 8 ft 1x2 and two 8 ft 1x6. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.
materials: 3/4" hardwood plywood or MDF, half sheet, 48 in x 48 in or larger

steps.title: Build the side panels
steps.body: Build three identical side panels by attaching 2x2 framing to the top and bottom of each plywood panel.
materials: 3/4" hardwood plywood or MDF, half sheet, 48 in x 48 in or larger

steps.title: Add drawer slide spacers
steps.body: From two of the three panels built in the previous step, attach 1x2 drawer slide spacers, making sure no screws stick out where the drawer slides will mount.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs to the two drawer-slide side panels.

steps.title: Add the shelf and top
steps.body: Attach the shelf and top to the two leg-and-drawer-slide panels.
materials: 3/4" hardwood plywood or MDF, half sheet, 48 in x 48 in or larger

steps.title: Add the back panel and trim
steps.body: Attach the third side panel to the back. Add 2x2 trim across the shelves and bottom.

steps.title: Add drawer trim
steps.body: Add the small trim piece between the two drawer openings.

steps.title: Build and install the drawers
steps.body: Build the drawer boxes from 1x6s around the plywood bottoms, sized to the opening you actually built rather than the plan dimension. Check your slide's clearance requirement and adjust drawer width to match. Attach the drawer slides and install.
materials: 3/4" hardwood plywood or MDF, half sheet, 48 in x 48 in or larger

steps.title: Build the doors and finish
steps.body: Build the door frames from 1x2 stock, test-fit with hinges, then mount the acrylic panels to the back with mirror clips. Install clasps to keep the doors shut. Sand and finish the whole table.
materials: Acrylic glass panels, 12 in x 12 in
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: End table with two drawers and mirrored acrylic doors
